channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: With their Los Angeles-area homes still smoldering, families return to search the ruins for memories
item.description: Some Los Angeles area residents have begun returning to their still smoldering neighborhoods to search for memories even as the threat of new fires persists.
item.dc:creator: Manuel Valdes, Julie Watson, John Seewer And Heather Hollingsworth, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Venezuela latest: Maduro sworn in for third term as global backlash and sanctions mount
item.description: CARACAS, Venezuela (AP) — Venezuelan President Nicolás Maduro was sworn in for a third six-year term Friday despite international condemnation of his recent reelection as illegitimate, as his administration grows increasingly brazen in cracking down on opponents.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Rare gold coin found in red kettle donations for The Salvation Army
item.description: A rare South African Krugerrand gold coin worth thousands was found amid the spare change donated to a Salvation Army red kettle in Colorado.
item.dc:creator: Jacob Factor, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Palisades Fire threatens Los Angeles' San Fernando Valley
item.description: LOS ANGELES (KTLA) - The Palisades Fire, the most destructive wildfire in Los Angeles history, edged closer to one of the city's major population centers on Friday: the San Fernando Valley.
item.dc:creator: Lily Dallow, KRON4

item.title: Former Miami Dolphins player arrested after crash in South Carolina
item.description: Former NFL player Robert Quinn was arrested in South Carolina following an early Friday morning crash.
item.dc:creator: Tim Renaud, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Americans have dimmer view of Biden than they did of Trump or Obama as term ends, AP-NORC poll finds
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — As Joe Biden prepares to leave office, Americans have a dimmer view of his presidency than they did at the end of Donald Trump's first term or Barack Obama's second, a new poll finds. Around one-quarter of U.S.
item.dc:creator: WILL WEISSERT and AMELIA THOMSON-DEVEAUX, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump assassination attempt victims 'honored' to attend 47th president's inauguration
item.description: Trump assassination attempt victims James Copenhaver and David Dutch, who were injured in the July shooting in Pennsylvania, will be attending the 47th president's inauguration.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Supreme Court seems likely to uphold law that would force TikTok to shut down on Jan. 19
item.description: The Supreme Court on Friday seemed likely to uphold a law that would ban TikTok in the United States beginning Jan. 19 unless the popular social media program is sold by its China-based parent company.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Man detained near Kenneth Fire not accused of arson
item.description: Editor's Note: This article has been updated and a video removed to clarify there were no arson charges. LOS ANGELES (KTLA) - A man who was detained by citizens shortly after a wildfire exploded near Calabasas and West Hills is not accused of starting the fire, authorities announced Friday morning.
item.dc:creator: Vivian Chow, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Man detained near Kenneth Fire not accused of arson
item.description: Editor's Note: This article has been updated and a video removed to clarify there were no arson charges. LOS ANGELES (KTLA) - A man who was detained by citizens shortly after a wildfire exploded near Calabasas and West Hills is not accused of starting the fire, authorities announced Friday morning.
item.dc:creator: Vivian Chow, Fox 5

item.title: Freedom Caucus members to huddle with Trump on strategy
item.description: Members of the House Freedom Caucus say they will make the case for their preferred policies and strategy when they meet with President-elect Trump at Mar-a-Lago on Friday to discuss his ambitious legislative agenda — bringing preferences that at times clash with those of Trump. Rep.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, NewsNation

item.title: Actor Steve Guttenberg returns to his wildfire-charred LA neighborhood
item.description: Steve Guttenberg awoke Thursday morning to a grim reality: The treacherous wildfire that tore through the Pacific Palisades had left his once-lush neighborhood charred and unrecognizable.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Illegal immigrant accused in attempted teen kidnapping deported 5 times: officials
item.description: A Mexican illegal immigrant deported from the United States five times has been arrested after allegedly attempting to kidnap a teenage Alabama girl on New Year's Eve.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Jamie Lee Curtis donates $1M to LA wildfire relief efforts
item.description: Jamie Lee Curtis says her family is donating $1 million to relief efforts amid the wildfires in California that have devastated the Los Angeles area.
item.dc:creator: Judy Kurtz, NewsNation

item.title: 7 notable moments between VIPs at Carter’s funeral
item.description: Former President Carter’s funeral service at Washington National Cathedral brought together all five living presidents and current and former officials for an event full of notable interactions.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, NewsNation

item.title: LA DA: 'Max punishment' will be sought for looters, arsonist
item.description: “Justice will be swift. It will be firm, and the maximum punishment will be sought,” Hochman said on "Banfield.
item.dc:creator: Liz Jassin, NewsNation

item.title: Wildfires latest: At least 10,000 structures have burned in a series of fires around Los Angeles
item.description: Firefighters battled Thursday to control a series of major fires in the Los Angeles area that have killed seven people, burned at least 10,000 structures from the Pacific Coast to Pasadena and sent thousands of people frantically fleeing their homes.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Jimmy Carter lauded for humility and service in Washington before being laid to rest in Georgia
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Jimmy Carter was celebrated Thursday for his personal humility and public service before, during and after his presidency in a funeral at Washington National Cathedral featuring the kind of pageantry the 39th U.S. president typically eschewed.
item.dc:creator: BILL BARROW and CHRIS MEGERIAN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Supreme Court rejects Trump’s bid to delay sentencing in his New York hush money case
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A sharply divided Supreme Court on Thursday rejected President-elect Donald Trump’s final bid to put his New York hush-money case on hold, clearing the way for him to be sentenced for felony crimes days before he returns to the presidency. The court’s 5-4 order allows Judge Juan M.
item.dc:creator: LINDSAY WHITEHURST, MICHAEL HILL and MICHAEL R. SISAK, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: In Los Angeles, people snap up air filters and wear masks against smoke pollution
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — With levels of sooty air pollution far too high across swaths of the Los Angeles area, Dana Benton is sleeping with a mask on, even with her air purifier running.
item.dc:creator: DORANY PINEDA and TAMMY WEBBER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Anita Bryant, anti-gay rights activist and singer, dead at 84
item.description: Anita Bryant Dry, known popularly Anita Bryant — a singer and crusader against the progress of gay rights — has died at the age of 84, her family announced in an obituary.
item.dc:creator: Russell Falcon, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Supreme Court rejects Trump’s bid to delay sentencing in his New York hush money case
item.description: A divided Supreme Court on Thursday rejected President-elect Donald Trump’s bid to delay his sentencing in his hush money case in New York.
item.dc:creator: LINDSAY WHITEHURST, MICHAEL HILL and MICHAEL R. SISAK Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: The Los Angeles landmarks from film, TV damaged by wildfires
item.description: The high school where Brian De Palma brought Stephen King’s “Carrie” to life, Will Rogers’ ranch house, and a motel owned by William Randolph Hearst are among some of the famous structures that have been damaged or destroyed by the California wildfires.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: As wildfires burn in LA, what to know about protective masks
item.description: Certain masks are effective in protecting against wildfire smoke — but only if they are fitted and worn properly.
item.dc:creator: Jeff Arnold, KRON4

item.title: A powerful winter storm's heavy snow and sleet cancels flights and closes schools across US South
item.description: DALLAS (AP) — A powerful winter storm brought heavy snow and icy conditions to parts of the U.S. South on Thursday, forcing officials to close schools, cancel flights and warn residents in some of the worst-hit areas to stay off roads as it lumbered eastward through Oklahoma and Texas. Arkansas Gov.
item.dc:creator: JAMIE STENGLE and KENDRIA LAFLEUR, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: LA Mayor Karen Bass stonewalls reporter for 90 seconds on wildfires
item.description: "Have you nothing to say today? Have you absolutely nothing to say to the citizens today," SkyNews reporter David Blevins pressed her.
item.dc:creator: Dominick Mastrangelo, NewsNation

item.title: Aerial video shows before and after views of Palisades Fire in Los Angeles
item.description: “I couldn’t image what we were preparing to see once we got over here on this side of the hill,” pilot Rich Prickett said.
item.dc:creator: Tony Kurzweil, NewsNation

item.title: LA Mayor Bass stonewalls reporter for 90 seconds at airport
item.description: Video taken on an airport jetway shows Los Angeles Mayor Karen Bass repeatedly ignoring questions from a reporter about her response to the wildfires ripping through the area this week.
item.dc:creator: Dominick Mastrangelo, KRON4

item.title: California fire victim dies holding hose, defending home
item.description: One of the five confirmed fatalities from the destructive Eaton Fire was identified on Wednesday morning when his family found his body on the side of the road by their home – with a garden hose still in his hand.
item.dc:creator: Lily Dallow, WFLA, NBC

item.title: All 5 living presidents, political dignitaries attend Jimmy Carter's funeral
item.description: Political leaders gathered to honor the life of former President Jimmy Carter during his state funeral at the Washington National Cathedral on Thursday morning.
item.dc:creator: Jordan Connell, WGN 9

item.title: Firefighters battle devastating Los Angeles wildfires as winds calm somewhat
item.description: EDITOR’S NOTE: Click HERE to view The Associated Press’ wildfire tracker, which shows where wildfires are spreading.
item.dc:creator: MANUEL VALDES, HALLIE GOLDEN and JULIE WATSON, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Biden eulogizes ‘great American’ Jimmy Carter
item.description: President Biden on Thursday eulogized former President Carter, hailing the 39th president as a man of character who left an enduring legacy both during his time in the White House and after leaving office.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, WGN 9

item.title: Trump says Newsom should resign as Los Angeles wildfires rage
item.description: The president-elect's criticism of Newsom has sparked a political battle over the fires that have killed at least five people and are far from contained.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, Fox 5

item.title: 4 in 10 companies planning job cuts due to AI: Survey
item.description: Companies across the U.S., at least four in 10, are likely to cut jobs as artificial intelligence (AI) continues to advance, new research shows.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, KRON4

item.title: How to safely evacuate during a wildfire
item.description: Evacuating a wildfire area can be scary, but experts say people can significantly improve their own safety with a little preparation and common sense.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Hollywood Hills blaze adds new danger to devastating LA fires
item.description: Combined, the fires have consumed about 22 square miles — about half the size of the entire city of San Francisco.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Knight, Fox 5

item.title: Payments go out in $100M Verizon settlement, but people are disappointed
item.description: Payments have finally started going out in the $100 million class action lawsuit Verizon agreed to settle, but the deposits are falling short of people's expectations.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, Fox 5
